member.name: Josh MacAlister

addresses: First Floor, 35-40 James Street, Whitehaven, CA28 7HZ

member.synopsis: Josh MacAlister is the Labour MP for Whitehaven and Workington , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently holds the Government post of Parliamentary Under-Secretary (Department for Education).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Solar Power: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, when his Department plans to publish its conclusions on the recommended (a) scales and (b) durations of Community Benefit Fund payments for (i) solar farms and (ii) associated Battery Energy
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 21 May, the government published a working paper on community benefits and shared ownership of low carbon energy infrastructure. The working paper intends to gather insight on proposals for mandatory community benefit scheme in Great Britain, including...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Bluetongue Disease: Disease Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps his Department is taking to support farmers in the north of England reliant on cross-border stock movements following the extension of the bluetongue virus restricted zone.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the honourable member to the answer given on 4 June 2025 to question UIN 57569. We continue to work closely with industry representatives to ensure that keepers are kept up to date with developments and that issues and concerns are addressed promp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Wales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Wales, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to support the steel industry in Wales.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Welsh steel has a bright future under this government. Our improved deal with Tata Steel UK has delivered £1.3 billion investment and protected over 5,000 jobs.We have committed a further £2.5 billion for steel over the course of this Parliament with our ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Wales Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Tennis: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to invest in tennis facilities following the end of the Park Tennis Project.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that everyone, regardless of background, should have access to and benefit from quality sport and physical activity opportunities.Sport England provides long term investment to the Lawn Tennis Association, the Natio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to increase the provision of mental health services in rural areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards are responsible for providing mental health services to meet the needs of their local populations.As part of our plan for change, we will reduce delays and provide faster treatment.We are working with NHS England to transform mental...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: National Nuclear Laboratory
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to ensure the UK’s National Nuclear Laboratory is able to conduct commercially beneficial research.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
